new_classes.label_en: Other Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22042298)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22042298)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes in containers of more than 2 litres, not bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) or Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), classified under CN subheading 22042298 (Chapter 22). This residual category covers all other wines in large containers falling under the 2204 22 range that do not qualify for a PDO or PGI designation.

coverage_explanation: The main discriminating criteria captured are: (1) still wine (non-sparkling, subclass of StillWine), (2) containers of more than 2 litres (decimalRange restriction on maxContainerVolumeL > 2), (3) residual/other category — no PDO or PGI (complement restrictions on hasGeographicOriginQuality). The legal note references additional notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, which specify technical criteria for the total dry extract and its non-volatile constituents (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, and salts). These analytical/compositional criteria are complex measurement thresholds used to discriminate certain wine types within the 22042222–22042298 range; they are not fully modelled here because no existing properties for individual dry-extract components (e.g. glycerol content, tannin content) exist in the TBox, and introducing them all would over-commit the ontology. The coverage is therefore partial.
